question_text: A student has three magnetic materials: a permanent magnet, an iron nail, and a copper wire wrapped around an iron core with current flowing through it. Which of the following correctly orders these by their ability to produce a magnetic field?

options.label: A
options.text: Permanent magnet > electromagnet > iron nail (no field)

options.label: B
options.text: Electromagnet > permanent magnet > iron nail (no field)

options.label: C
options.text: Permanent magnet and electromagnet (both strong) > iron nail (very weak field only near the permanent magnet)

explanation: Both permanent magnets and electromagnets produce strong, independent magnetic fields. The iron nail only becomes magnetic when placed near a permanent magnet (induced magnetism), so it has no field of its own. The electromagnet can be switched on and off, whereas the permanent magnet's field is always present.
